title: Sales related occupations n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the selling, buying, leasing, or governance activities of commercial, industrial, or residential real estate properties. Includes managers of homeowner and condominium associations, rented or leased housing units, buildings, or land (including rights-of-way). Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. (Based on 50 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Plan work schedules and keep records of employees' work schedules and time cards.
Inventory stock and reorder when it drops to a specified level.
Plan budgets, authorize payments, and manage merchandise returns.
Establish credit policies, operating procedures, and formulate pricing policies for merchandise according to profitability requirements.
Confer with company officials to develop methods and procedures to increase sales, expand markets, and promote business.
Plan and coordinate advertising campaigns and sales promotions, preparing merchandise displays and advertising copy.
Review inventory and sales records to prepare reports for management and budget departments.
Examine merchandise to ensure accurate pricing, display, and functionality as advertised.
Estimate consumer demand and determine types and amounts of goods to be sold.
Instruct staff on handling difficult sales and establish policies and procedures for the department.
Perform work activities of subordinates, including sales, inventory-taking, and customer service.
Hire, train, and evaluate sales or marketing personnel, promoting or dismissing when appropriate.
Examine products purchased for resale or received for storage to assess their condition.
Keep records of purchases, sales, and requisitions; enforce safety and security rules in sales management.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales, inventory, and customer service activities.
Monitor sales activities to ensure satisfactory customer service and quality goods.
Provide customer service by greeting and assisting customers, addressing inquiries and complaints.
Assign employees specific duties and guide them in operational processes.
Advise clients on market conditions, prices, legal requirements, and financial strategies.
Sell products, maintain records of sales, and generate lists of compatible properties for clients.
Prepare and present purchase offers, legal documents, and interpret financial information for clients.
Conduct periodic account reviews, advising clients on necessary changes due to life or economic changes.
Promote sales of properties through advertisements and networking; manage client relationships.
Coordinate property showings and advise sellers on making homes more appealing to buyers.
Inspect properties to assess condition, recommend maintenance, and ensure compliance with regulations.
Generate lists of available properties and promote them to potential buyers and tenants.
Coordinate negotiations between buyers and sellers and finalize contract agreements for property transactions.
Manage financial aspects of the property, including collections and financial reporting.
Evaluate rental markets and adjust rents accordingly; oversee compliance with environmental regulations.
Rent or lease properties, acting as an intermediary to negotiate favorable terms for both buyers and sellers.
Maintain knowledge of real estate laws and market trends to inform clients accurately.
Respond to client inquiries regarding financial planning and property management needs.

Perform office duties, such as maintaining staff records, billing patients, receiving payments, or answering customer inquiries. Assist individuals in filling out applications or questionnaires and provide information about rental items, such as availability, operation, or description. Greet customers and ascertain what each customer wants or needs, and confer with customers to determine their service requirements and preferences.
Prepare reports showing problems encountered, answers to specific customer inquiries, or places of departure and destination, including relevant data analysis.

Prepare proposals, quotes, contracts, or presentations for potential customers, ensuring compliance with various requests and determining the suitability of products and services. Provide customers with information regarding payments and incentive options, warranties, or contracts to support service agreements and assist in inventory processes.

Monitor inventory levels, advise customers on product usage, recommend various products and services based on customer needs, and assist in planning marketing strategies by studying market trends and customer behavior.

Establish or maintain cooperative relationships with representatives of community, consumer, or public interest groups while conducting market or public opinion research to identify new business opportunities and trends. Prepare and deliver communications to further public relations objectives and promote products.
Align customer services and manage customer accounts, including providing travel suggestions, making reservations, and confirming details to incorporate customer preferences in sales initiatives.

Prepare sales slips or sales contracts, compute sales prices, total purchases, and ensure accurate payment processing. Regularly maintain knowledge of current sales promotions and maintain records related to transactions and customer interactions.

Assist customers in troubleshooting product issues and recommend alternative solutions or modifications when necessary. Maintain communication to ensure optimal customer satisfaction and address any concerns that arise during the sales or service process.

Demonstrate equipment and explain operational features to customers, ensuring clear understanding of products and services available, and assist in hands-on demonstrations to promote customer engagement and satisfaction.
Keep work areas clean and organized, overseeing transactions and responding to customer complaints to ensure a positive retail environment.

Prepare, deliver, and store merchandise, ensuring that items are readily available for customer requests and inquiries. Answer questions regarding the store and its merchandise while assisting with inventory management for effective stock control.
Inspect and adjust rental items according to customer needs, handle inquiries regarding rental fees, reserve items as requested, and maintain records of transactions.
Conduct public appearances or promotional events to increase product awareness, demonstrating effective marketing practices to enhance customer engagement and support sales initiatives.
Provide after-sale support by troubleshooting customer issues and ensuring warranty coverage or repair services are adequately addressed, maintaining customer relationships post-purchase.
Watch for and recognize security risks to ensure a safe retail environment, implementing measures to mitigate potential security breaches.
Collect payment for sales, prepare customer invoices, and handle financial transactions with accuracy to support business operations.
Negotiate prices or terms of sales or service agreements, including contracts and payments.
Collaborate with colleagues to exchange information, such as selling strategies or marketing information.
Maintain customer records using automated systems to support sales activities.
Answer questions about product features, benefits, prices, availability, or credit terms.
Circulate among potential customers or travel to deliver or sell merchandise or services, including promotional materials.
Develop prospect lists and identify prospective customers using various methods such as business directories or participation in organizations.
Distribute product samples or literature detailing products or services and stock resources such as promotional materials.
Order or purchase supplies and manage inventory as needed.
Set up and display sample merchandise at events or sales stands.
Prepare sales presentations or proposals to explain product specifications or applications, including cost estimates for product manufacturing or services.
Provide customers with ongoing technical support and consult with clients after sales to resolve problems.
Prepare sales contracts and order forms, including submitting sales orders for processing.
Contact new or existing customers to discuss how specific products or services can meet their needs and provide ongoing support.
Select or assist customers in selecting products based on their needs and applicable regulations.
Visit establishments to evaluate needs or promote product or service sales, including performance demonstrations.
Persuade customers to purchase merchandise or services and recommend products based on customer needs and interests.
Review market conditions, product innovations, competitors' products, and sales strategies to inform sales approaches.
Complete administrative duties such as preparing sales budgets and reports, and maintain accurate transaction records.
Contact prospective customers to present information about available services and explain pricing or contract terms.
Track and analyze market conditions and develop marketing strategies to compete effectively.
Prepare invoices or cost quotations for services rendered and arrange for payment of fees or charges.
Verify proper packaging and labeling of exported goods and ensure documentation adheres to customs and regulatory requirements.
Provide detailed port information, including shipment status notifications to clients involved in import/export.
Inform investment decisions by analyzing financial information to forecast business, industry, or economic conditions.
Negotiate shipping rates with freight carriers and arrange transport of goods with shipping or freight companies, considering factors such as costs and environmental impact.
Prepare and maintain shipping documentation, ensuring accuracy and compliance with regulations, while keeping records of all goods shipped, received, and stored.
Maintain current knowledge of relevant legislation, political situations, or other factors affecting shipping and logistics operations.
Evaluate business operations and recommend improvements, focusing on efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and risk management in shipping and logistic activities.
Analyze data to evaluate customer needs and respond to inquiries, ensuring high-quality client communication and support.
Coordinate and oversee packing, shipping, and delivery processes to ensure timely and efficient operations, while supervising staff involved in these activities.
Develop promotional materials and sales literature for clients, focusing on effective advertising strategies and client engagement.
Conduct market research to identify new advertising opportunities and contribute to the development of promotional plans and sales strategies.
Send samples of clients' work and promotional materials to potential employers or partners to gain new contracts, sponsorships, or endorsements.
Gather all relevant materials and coordinate bidding processes for potential contracts, ensuring all documentation is complete for approval.
Write sales outlines and other documentation as needed to assist staff in their sales activities.
Confer with clients to develop strategies for their careers, explain actions taken on their behalf, and arrange meetings concerning issues involving their clients.
Maintain assigned account bases while developing new accounts and keep informed of industry trends and deals.
Collect fees, commissions, or other payments according to contract terms.
Develop contacts with individuals and organizations and apply effective strategies and techniques to ensure their clients' success.

Schedule promotional or performance engagements for clients, conduct auditions or interviews to evaluate potential clients, and negotiate with managers, promoters, union officials, and others regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to new and existing customers to sell new advertising programs and to protect and increase existing advertising.
Assess cost effectiveness of products, projects, or services, track actual costs relative to bids as the project develops, and prepare cost estimates for use in selecting vendors or subcontractors.

Conduct special studies to develop and establish standard hour and related cost data or to reduce cost; review material and labor requirements to decide whether it is more cost-effective to produce or purchase components; and prepare cost and expenditure statements and other necessary documentation at regular intervals for the duration of the project.

Consult with clients, vendors, personnel in other departments, or construction foremen to discuss and formulate estimates and resolve issues, and confer with engineers, architects, owners, contractors, and subcontractors on changes and adjustments to cost estimates.
Prepare invoices, shipping documents, and contracts, and ensure that money collected is properly recorded and secured.

Collect payment for merchandise, record transactions, and send items, such as checks or money orders for further processing; obtain customers' names, addresses, billing information, product numbers, and specifications of items to be purchased; and calculate total charges for merchandise or services and shipping charges.

Monitor or track sustainability indicators and assess or propose sustainability initiatives; create marketing or outreach media to communicate sustainability issues, procedures, or objectives; and prepare reports or presentations to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives.

Provide customer with brochures and publications containing travel information, such as local customs, points of interest, or foreign country regulations, and book transportation and hotel reservations, using computer or telephone.

Develop core analytical capabilities or model libraries, using advanced statistical, quantitative, or econometric techniques, and promote products in online communities through weblog or discussion-forum postings or online advertising.
Review orders for completeness according to reporting procedures and forward incomplete orders for further processing, and check inventory records for availability of requested merchandise.
Inspect outgoing work for compliance with customers' specifications and verify customer and order information for correctness, checking it against previously obtained information as necessary.

Create descriptions of merchandise for posting to online storefronts, auction sites, or other shopping websites, and analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends, patterns, or warnings indicating threats to security of people, assets, information, or infrastructure.
Gather records pertinent to specific problems, review them for completeness and accuracy, and maintain files and control records to show correspondence activities.

Attend professional meetings, read literature, and participate in training or other educational offerings to keep abreast of new developments and technologies related to disaster recovery and business continuity.
Respond to emergency situations on an on-call basis and create business continuity and disaster recovery budgets.
Create or administer training and awareness presentations or materials and compose descriptions of merchandise for posting to online storefronts, auction sites, or other shopping websites.
Determine and set product prices, recommend improvements in sales procedures, and develop business plans while monitoring sales staff performance to ensure goals are met.

Correspond with customers via email or telephone to address inquiries and resolve complaints regarding products and services, while delivering transaction confirmations and maintaining records of purchases, sales, and customer accounts.

Analyze details of sales territories and conduct market research to identify opportunities for growth, while participating in promotional activities and preparing marketing materials to optimize product exposure.
Review merchandise for condition, ensure correct pricing and display, and coordinate promotional activities to enhance customer engagement and sales.
Order or purchase merchandise to maintain optimal inventory levels, inventory stock, and manage shipping supplies, ensuring effective supply chain management.
Create and manage orders or invoices using order management software and process payments from customers through electronic transaction services.
Train employees and develop work schedules, ensuring effective task assignment and performance management within the sales environment.
Plan and prepare online marketing strategies and customer interfaces to maximize engagement while collaborating with other team members to coordinate business activities.
Attend company meetings to exchange information related to product and business operations, focusing on strategies to expand markets and increase sales.
Implement measures for enhancing worker motivation and service quality, while monitoring performance and addressing issues related to staff and customer interactions.
Prepare promotional material and coordinate sales activities, including examining product condition and pricing, to ensure successful marketing and presentation of merchandise.
Explain and demonstrate tasks to new workers and ensure compliance with safety rules and regulations within the team environment.
Negotiate and prepare rental or lease agreements for equipment and machinery, specifying charges and payment procedures, to facilitate business operations.
Examine products for resale and complete all necessary documentation related to rental agreements and financial transactions for accurate record keeping.
Create and distribute offline promotional materials to support the sales strategy and customer engagement efforts.
Conduct customer interface improvements and analyze web site usage data to refine online platforms for better customer access and product marketing.
Monitor sales activities to ensure compliance with company procedures and correct discrepancies in pricing or product display.
Formula pricing policies according to profitability while preparing reports for management and budget departments.
